legal_definition.text: Sickle Cell Disease is a hereditary hemoglobin disorder characterized by abnormal sickle-shaped red blood cells, leading to anemia, pain crises, infections, and organ damage.
legal_definition.statutory_source: Schedule to the Rights of Persons with Disabilities Act, 2016

statutory_status.act: Rights of Persons with Disabilities Act, 2016

benchmark_requirement.definition: Person with benchmark disability means a person with not less than forty per cent of a specified disability as certified.
benchmark_requirement.assessment_note: Severity assessed based on frequency of crises, organ involvement, anemia severity, and functional limitation as per notified medical guidelines.

reservation_category.grouping_logic: Included under locomotor disability and specified blood disorders cluster.
reservation_category.eligibility_condition: Reservation applicable only for benchmark disability.

certification_workflow.assessment_guidelines: Evaluation as per Central Government notified medical guidelines under Section 56.

reasonable_accommodation_examples: Access to medical leave and emergency support

distinction_framework: Sickle Cell Disease is classified as a blood disorder under the Schedule.
It remains legally distinct though grouped for reservation computation.
Reservation grouping does not merge statutory identities.
legal_boundary_conditions: Reservation applicable only for benchmark disability.
Assessment must follow notified blood disorder evaluation norms.
interpretive_notes: Sickle Cell Disease severity varies significantly among individuals.
Recurrent pain crises and organ damage may produce substantial functional impairment.
Legal protection applies based on certified functional impact.

legal_schema.law_name: Rights of Persons with Disabilities Act
